idea.hook: Thinking about a cosmic road trip to an entire galaxy cluster?
facts: The Virgo Cluster, our closest large galaxy cluster, is ~54 million light-years away.
Even at light speed (299,792 km/s), it would take 54 million years to reach it.
Our fastest spacecraft, New Horizons, cruises at ~58,536 km/h.
At this speed, a journey to the Virgo Cluster would last over 995 BILLION years.
idea.payoff: That's ~72x longer than the Universe has existed! Truly intergalactic travel remains a sci-fi dream.
idea.title: Visit A Galaxy Cluster?! 🤯🌌

scenes.text: Cosmic Road Trip to the Virgo Cluster?
scenes.visual: A swirling galaxy background, distant stars slowly drifting.
Starts with a close-up of our Milky Way (stylized), then zooms out rapidly to show it as a tiny speck, revealing a distant, larger cluster of galaxies (Virgo Cluster representation).

scenes.text: Even at LIGHT SPEED: 54 Million Years
scenes.visual: A bright light beam streaks across a starfield. Numbers '1' then '54,000,000' appear with a 'YEARS' label.
An image of the New Horizons spacecraft zooms in briefly, then a massive number '995,000,000,000' appears and morphs into '>995 BILLION YEARS'.

scenes.text: 72x Older Than The Universe Itself. Intergalactic Travel: Still a Sci-Fi Dream.
scenes.visual: A breathtaking view of multiple distant galaxies, swirling gently, with a small, reflective human silhouette gazing out into the vastness. The final text flashes subtly.
